title: Maria
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Maria}} {{POI|name=Maria Holdings|status=Alive?|location=[[The Backrooms]]|affiliations=possibly [[Caius Taggert]]|seen=|image=[[File:CUIcon.png|right|120px|link=Persons of Interest]]}} __NOTOC__ {{VoidedTheme|mono}} {{Blurimage|file=Self_Portrait_of_Maria.jpg|caption=Fig. I: Self-portrait.}} == Description == '''Maria Holdings''', often simply referred to as '''Maria''', is a female nomadic [[Entity 9|faceling]] child of about seven years old, who has often been observed roaming the Backrooms by many wanderers. She has a single eye, dark hair and an incomplete mouth, and wears monochrome clothing. Apparently, she presents with an anomalous lack of biological ageing; since 1997, witness accounts of Maria have asserted that she does not seem to mature with the years. Alongside her apparently eternal biological youth, Maria does not seem to have aged mentally beyond the capacity of a seven-year-old either. She expresses frequent curiosity towards humans, other [[Entity 9|facelings]], and [[glowfolk]], and often follows wanderers simply to observe their actions. In addition, she has often been known to play practical jokes on wanderers, including writing on their faces or clothing, or stacking their belongings into tower-like structures. Although she is not known to have exhibited aggression towards humans, caution is nonetheless advised, as child facelings are known to attack out of fear or self-defence. Maria's voicebox is not fully dysfunctional; she has been known to utter full words and short phrases under some circumstances, which she presumably learned from her father. Personal details which she has revealed through writing include that she hates citrus fruits and [[Entity 25|counter-entities]], loves sweet foods, and has an odd case of globophobia which she puts down to ''"the sound of [balloons] popping"''. Despite her seeming immaturity, Maria appears to be quite experienced in navigating the Backrooms and dealing with its myriad dangers alone. She is known to leave behind childish drawings in dangerous [[levels]] to warn wanderers of hazards; while most if not all of these provide a reasonable warning to wanderers, it is important to remember that she may overlook or mislabel certain hazards due to her entitic nature. Provided below is a gallery of Maria's sketches. Figure II=

 == Past == Maria's father was Tobias Murellus Holdings,Tobias Holdings was one of the Sixty Speakers of Facelessdom, the sixty known facelings who were able to utilise their vocal cords for speech in the 1980s. There are only fifty-eight Speakers known to be living at present, some of whom were not of the original Sixty. a genius inventor who was manipulated into the service of [[Caius Taggert]] under the guise of a business partnership. After realising the Party Host's deception, he devised a mechanism which would mimic the effects of [[Phenomenon 106|Belphegor's Prime]], and lured Sir Taggert into a false circus tent of sacrificial faceling performers, whereupon Holdings initiated a Closed-Circuit Firestorm and plunged the tent into fire. Maria, who had crept into the circus tent against her father's warnings, was consumed by the flames. Somehow, in some way, both [[Caius Taggert]] and Maria returned from those flames. Yet by the time Maria emerged back into the world from the great stasis of death which she had been in, the Party Host had already done away with her father for good. Her rise from the dead, it would seem, brought her eternal youth and a quiet, curious sort of wisdom which is not often seen in children of her age, of whatever sapient race they might be. == Affiliations == {{Blurimage|file=Caius and Maria.jpg|caption=Fig. XV: Witness sketch of Maria and Sir Taggert by the [[The Party Pooper|Party Pooper]].}} === [[Caius Taggert]] === Maria's relationship with the Party Host is uncertain. According to Sir Taggert, Maria occasionally attends his parties and revels, and is immune to the [[Sanguine Festivus|Sanguine Festivus Virus]], although she disapproves of the immense bloodshed of the [[Sanguine Festivus|partygoers]]' cannibalistic rituals. Eyewitnesses have observed her gifting hand-drawn sketches to the Party Host — but whether this is out of fearful respect or affection is unknown. Maria herself expresses presumed indifference at the mention of the Party Host. When requested to comment on the relationship between Maria and himself, Sir Taggert replied, ''"That little [[Entity 9|faceling]]? I haven't seen her in a while. She used to draw me the most adorable pictures, y'know. Always ran at the sight of my balloon, afraid it would pop or sumthin'. A cute littl'un, that marionette... Ahem. No. I've never, y'know, been fond of her or anythin'. No, nothin' of the sort. Ahem. No signs of affection, no nothin'. Nope, none at all."'' This slightly flustered string of sentences was followed by an unwillingness to elaborate and the immediate death of the unfortunate interviewer. === The "Keyman" === Several of Maria's drawings depict an unascertained entity she refers to as the "Keyman". This individual apparently "has keys", is "cool" and "grumpy but nice", and "won't let [Maria] eat his keys". [[Caius Taggert]], when asked to comment on this unknown figure, simply stated that ''"he is no longer one to be considered; he never should have been anyway. A dark thing he was, indeed, yet young Maria still found a way to love him and his idiot keys somehow."'' Maria herself has not provided much explanation for these drawings beyond the aforementioned keywords, but there are several hypotheses suggesting that the entity depicted may actually be the [[Juju Man]]. ----
